purpose: settings.static_graph_facts=false: the generated graph-attribute facts are stamped non-static (graphml_parser.py:60/90 via pyreason.py:603, consumed at load time), so with persistent=false the special/rel bounds are reset to [0,1] at the start of every t>0 (interpretation.py:260-273) — the rule grounds only at t=0, derived(B) appears at t=1 only, and the perfect-convergence exit fires at t=1 (interpretation.py:674-680), so get_time reads 2 with two frames per filter probe, where the twin static-graph-facts-on re-derives derived(B) at t=2 and reads get_time 3 (verified at authoring — the harness judges each case within one knob position) — the nondefault-false-fluent, read-at-load-time and interaction-persistent classes.
provenance: spine case against docs/surface.md setting:static_graph_facts nondefault-false-fluent + read-at-load-time + interaction-persistent
